content_en:
A short but profound treatise by the great hadith master and historian al-Khatib al-Baghdadi — author of Tarikh Baghdad (History of Baghdad), one of the most important biographical dictionaries in Islamic scholarship. This text addresses the critical relationship between Islamic knowledge and its application in life.

Al-Khatib al-Baghdadi marshals Quranic verses, prophetic hadiths, and statements of the Salaf to establish that acquiring Islamic knowledge carries with it an obligation to act upon what one learns. The scholar who learns but does not act is more blameworthy than the ignorant person who sins, because knowledge increases the obligation and the proof against one on the Day of Judgment.

This work serves as a reminder for students of knowledge that the goal of Islamic learning is not mere intellectual accumulation but transformation of character and action in accordance with what Allah revealed. It is a small book with a large impact on Islamic educational tradition.
